description:
The default `--timeout-ms 5000` is too tight for mutation runs on functions
touched by many supplemental tests, particularly in Phoenix/Ecto apps where
test setup (DataCase sandbox checkout, Mox expectations, PubSub subscribes)
inflates per-mutation cycle time.

## Reproduction

From the atlas mutagen efficacy study (2026-05-18):

- File: `lib/atlas/kb/knowledge_base.ex`
- Mutations: 2 `pipeline` sites on Ecto changesets
- Result: both sites **timed out at 5s**
- Root cause: the host function is touched by ~12 supplemental tests across
  multiple modules, so ExUnit cycle time per mutation exceeded the budget.
- Adjudication: not a real test gap — mutagen just didn't have enough time
  to know whether the mutation was killed or survived.

## Why this matters

Timeouts are reported as `aborted` runs in the JSON. Adopters interpreting
the JSON have to either:
- treat aborts as failures (false positives — mutation might have been
  killed quickly with more budget)
- treat aborts as passes (false negatives — masks real gaps)
- raise `--timeout-ms` per run (requires per-project tuning, easy to
  forget, no documentation guidance)

A bad default produces confusing first-run experiences for adopters,
exactly the wrong signal for a tool being pitched as "works on real apps."

## Options (pick one or stack)

1. **Raise default to 30000 (30s)** for everyone. Simple, slight cost on
   small libs where mutations actually do finish in <5s.
2. **Document prominently in README** so adopters know to set
   `--timeout-ms 30000` on first run against a Phoenix/Ecto codebase.
3. **Make timeout config-driven** via a `.mutagen.exs` per-project config
   or a `config :mutagen_ex, timeout_ms: 30_000` Mix config.
4. **Auto-detect app-shaped projects** (Phoenix dep present, Ecto dep
   present in deps tree) and auto-raise to 30s in that case. Logs a
   one-liner noting the auto-raise.

Option 4 + option 2 (docs) is the most polished combination. Option 1 alone
is the smallest change.

## Evidence

- Atlas study Finding 3:
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/atlas/2026-05-18-efficacy-study.md
- Cross-project synthesis Finding 4.4

## Acceptance

- A re-run of the atlas study against `lib/atlas/kb/knowledge_base.ex`
  produces non-aborted outcomes for both pipeline mutations (regardless
  of whether the new default is high enough, or auto-raise kicked in,
  or the README told the user to set it).
- A re-run of the mutagen_ex v1 study sandboxes still finishes in
  reasonable time — no regression on small-lib performance.
title: Default --timeout-ms too tight for app-shaped projects (Phoenix/Ecto)
